what_it_does: Aggregates five broader research threads (B1–B5) into a single landscape document that evaluates whether the concurrent-cognition design is correct, how far the mechanism can reach, what model-configuration layer is missing, and how the work should be sequenced.
observations: Frames the concurrent-cognition shape as occupying an unclaimed cell across three design axes: parallel-for-quality, one-model-many-roles, and interact-during-a-streamed-part-reply.

Surveys external work including Compound AI Systems, Mixture-of-Agents, Self-MoA, Blackboard/Hearsay-II, Skeleton-of-Thought, Branch-Solve-Merge, and Chunked-cascade/Moshi, mapping each to a claim or risk.
Lists six proposed shape corrections such as rank-then-fuse, conditional staging, gate-weak-outputs, and mining blackboard control theory.
Diagnoses node-mechanism gaps: hardcoded role registry in suite.py, untyped edges, decorative output_schema, free-string ports, and a serial scheduler.
Maps fifteen applications and five emphasized reuses, plus four findings (R1–R4) that reshape the core mechanism.
Proposes a MODEL_CAPABILITIES registry keyed by model-id with provenance levels (declared/probed/measured/served) and a three-way join with service deployment and telemetry.
Argues that 32-way parallel cognition does not imply 32-way parallel build, and proposes one serial spine (S0–S6 on suite.py/bridge.py) plus three genuinely-disjoint parallel lanes.
Ends with ten net design deltas and an explicit set of decisions still owned by Tim.
standouts: The central spike risk is named: can a 4B aggregator fuse 32 role-outputs as well as a 110B aggregator?
The build plan is deliberately serial-spine-first, gated by the slot scheduler and the 4B-aggregator spike before any fan-out.
Per-draw variation is identified as a missing primitive, because a non-volatile memo can silently make a jury of N return one answer N times.
A model-capability registry is proposed as a join of intrinsic capability, service deployment, and telemetry rather than a single flat config.
conventions: Uses numbered sections and a comparison table to structure research synthesis.
Labels threads, findings, and build stages with short codes (B1–B5, R1–R4, S0–S6) for traceability.
References sibling documents (`../00-LANDSCAPE.md`, B1–B5) without reproducing them.
Combines literature review, design critique, mechanism diagnosis, application mapping, and build planning in one file.
Closes with an explicit owner-gated decision list ('Still Tim's to author').
concerns: The density of claims, proposed registries, and action items may be hard to track without issue links or identifiers.
Many proposed abstractions (edge kinds, activation-contexts, output_destination taxonomy) are declared but not detailed enough to implement directly.
External citations are named but lack URLs, dates, or versions, making independent verification harder.
Project-specific terms such as 'swarm://-addresses', 'mode-dial', and 'altitude-translation layer' are used without being defined in this file.
The prescribed serial-spine build order assumes a single branch and may conflict with team capacity or branching constraints.
notes: A dense wave-2 research synthesis that argues the concurrent-cognition design is novel, lists ten concrete deltas, and pins the build plan to a single serial spine plus three parallel lanes.
questions: What are the exact contents and structure of the per-thread B1–B5 documents?
What is the current implementation state of suite.py, bridge.py, nodes/llm.py, and the canvas/app render FE?
How is 'concurrency_knee' measured or probed in practice?
What criteria determine whether a role output is weak and should be gated before fusion?
What is the precise scope of each activation-context (presence, background, sense, rollup)?
Which of the ten net design deltas are partially implemented versus entirely net-new?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an aggregated landscape that evaluates design correctness, reach, model configuration, and build coordination, exactly as its opening paragraph states.
apparent_themes: Composition and system structure can outperform raw model scale.
Mechanism over heuristics: typed edges, registries, and data-driven capability joins.
Conditional execution and just-in-time staging reduce unnecessary overhead.
High-blast-radius code should be built through a serial, spike-gated spine.
Shared substrate with thin, divergent drivers for app and cognition paths.
Self-instrumenting and introspective closure as an organizing principle.
intention_signals: Establish a research-backed design position for concurrent cognition while translating that position into concrete registries, typed mechanisms, and a sequenced build plan that can gate the next loop-prep triad.
novelty: It frames the design as occupying a novel cell in a three-axis design space and derives build order from code blast-radius rather than parallelizing all cognition work at once.
contribution: A compact decision-ready reference consisting of the ten-item net design delta list, the model-capability registry concept, the serial-spine-plus-lanes build plan, the per-draw-variation primitive, and the explicit owner-gated open decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Aggregated wave-2 landscape document for concurrent cognition that evaluates external architectures, diagnoses node-mechanism gaps, maps fifteen applications, proposes a model-capability registry, and defines a serial-spine-plus-three-lanes build plan with ten net design deltas.
intention_for_embedding: To converge broader research and design critique into a common reference that gates the loop-prep triad for the concurrent-cognition build.
suggested_fields: decision_owner for each net delta and open item
implementation_state for each proposed registry or abstraction
proposed_edge_kinds: validates (external work validates a design claim)
gates (spike or prerequisite gates a build step)
diverges-from (app driver vs cognition driver over shared substrate)
joins-with (capability registry joins service deployment and telemetry)
proves (measurement or spike proves a risk)
